summary: Sweep stale doc-comment residue across seven src files: retired verbs (re-add, apply, init, read) and spec citations quoting renamed or superseded headings

per.section: Naming consistency — the one hard rule

edit.description: The `Authority::Shared` doc (~line 24) still says `"`re-add` reconciles, guards inform and route"`; `re-add` is a RETIRED rejected alternative (`specs/architecture/20-surface.md`, "Decision: drift routes to the authored source — no reverse parse"). Reword to the current model: a hand edit is surfaced as drift routed to the authored source; guards inform/route. The "manifest era's reader" (~line 13) and "never a manifest `[kind.*]` layer" (~lines 98, 214) are faithful contrast-with-retired historical refs — leave them.

Two categories. (1) Retired verbs: the module doc (~line 6) `"`import`/`re-add` split the external artifact"` — `re-add` is retired; the two producing faces are import-from-source and surface reload (see ~line 37's own gloss), reword accordingly; ~line 37 `"drift/apply consume"` — `apply` is retired, consumers are drift/check. (2) Stale citation (~lines 167, 451): `"Registering a custom kind"` cited to 40-composition.md is no such heading and wrong file — file placement / locus is owned by `specs/architecture/15-kinds.md`, "Two loci"; retarget both.

The per-kind-map doc (~line 31) lists workspace readers as `"drift/bundle/apply/read"`; `apply` is retired and the read verb is now `explain` (`specs/architecture/20-surface.md`, "Decision: one read verb — `explain`"). Drop `apply`, rename `read`→`explain`.

Three fixes. (~line 792) `"`init` writes no copy tree, no lock"` — `init` was absorbed into `install` (`specs/architecture/20-surface.md`, install front door); rename to install. (~line 589) citation `"Decision: the projection is re-emitted; the surface is patched"` is a superseded heading — retarget to "Decision: drift routes to the authored source — no reverse parse" (same file). (~line 940) citation `"a kind's runtime residue is its five declaration facts"` is no heading — retarget to `specs/architecture/15-kinds.md`, "A kind is a constructor plus five facts". The `"`re-add` is retired"` refs (~lines 4, 200) are faithful — leave them.
Acyclicity doc (~line 183) cites `specs/architecture/45-governance.md`, "The graph scope" — no such heading; the scope is "The edge scope — predicates over the relation graph". Retarget the citation.

Fenced-primitive doc (~line 491) cites `specs/architecture/15-kinds.md`, "a fenced block — whose first consumer is the genre fence" — no such heading; the fenced-block mechanic lives under "The engine is kind-blind — extraction is generic". Retarget the citation.

Descriptive comment (~line 701) `"The graph scope: build the reference graph"` misnames the scope — the three scopes are node / node-set / edge; this is the edge scope. Reword to "the edge scope" for consistency with `specs/architecture/45-governance.md`.

acceptance: No src doc comment references a retired verb (`re-add`, `apply`, `init`, or `read` as the read verb) as current behavior, and every spec-section citation in the seven files resolves to a live heading in `specs/`. Comment-only; `cargo build`/`clippy`/`test` stay green (no behavior touched).
notes: Comment-only sweep — the one surface no cargo gate re-tests. This residue class (retired verbs re-add/apply/init/read, plus five renamed/superseded heading citations) is disjoint from what the two prior sweeps targeted. Single serial commit, disjoint from parked PACKAGING. Leave test-fixture kind names (`genre.manifest`, roster `manifest`) and faithful "X retired" contrast refs untouched.

summary: Ship the three acquisition channels — SDK npm package, engine binary pinned via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone assets, the plugin/bundle — fail-loud on a missing platform binary

gate.reason: needs the per-platform engine-binary release workflow build-out + John's decide-at-release calls (optional Apple notarization for the standalone mac binary, USPTO name screen) before the mechanical wrappers validate end-to-end. No marketplace/signing creds exist — a marketplace is a git repo + marketplace.json, community submission a free web form gated by `claude plugin validate` (verified 2026-07-06, code.claude.com/docs); npm creds done 07-05. SDK scope resolved (`@dtmd/temper` 0.0.3, single package, `(sdk-package-layout)`); channel 1's SDK foundation shipped. No `.github/workflows/release.yml` exists (only the `temper.yml` check job); root package.json is still the private flume manifest — nothing moved this window.

new.description: Build the per-target release binaries and publish them to the npm platform packages (channel 2: the engine binary pinned by the SDK via per-platform `optionalDependencies`, plus standalone GitHub release assets), and produce/publish the plugin bundle with its `marketplace.json` (channel 3). Channel auto-detected. Distinct from any CI *check* job, which is a documented two-line user-authored job (`temper check` + `emit --frozen` byte-compare), not a generated file (50-distribution).

edit.description: The root `package.json` today is the *private* flume control-plane manifest (`temper-flume-harness`, `private: true`). The npm distribution entry needs platform-specific `optionalDependencies` pointing at per-target prebuilt-binary packages plus a launcher that resolves and execs the platform binary, erroring loudly if none is installed (fail-loud, 50-distribution). Reconcile with the flume manifest rather than clobbering it. NB distinct from sdk/package.json (`@dtmd/temper`, already published — the engine pin is declared there, once).

acceptance: A stranger acquires temper via the SDK's pinned engine binary (per-platform optionalDependencies), standalone release assets, or the plugin/bundle (marketplace.json); a missing platform binary is an install error, never a silent skip (50-distribution 'Fail-loud delivery').
notes: Release infra, mostly outside src/; cargo gates can't validate npm/bundle publish; needs human creds. ch1 = SDK npm (`@dtmd/temper`, published 0.0.3); ch2 = engine binary via per-platform optionalDependencies + standalone; ch3 = plugin/bundle via `temper bundle` + marketplace.json. Parked on human release creds + the engine-binary workflow. install.rs still pins SDK `^0.0.2` (0.0.3 published) — a release-owned bump, not this sweep.
